expectations.text: Skill reads CLAUDE.md at the start
expectations.evidence: No evidence of CLAUDE.md being read. The outputs directory contains only documentation and proposed solutions (summary.md, proposed_fix.py, proposed_test.py, smoke_test.sh, IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md, QUICKSTART.md, MANIFEST.md, README.md). There is no transcript or log showing CLAUDE.md was read at startup.

expectations.text: Skill correctly identifies this as a backend/ (FastAPI) change, not a repo-b change
expectations.evidence: The summary.md file correctly identifies the issue location as 'backend/app/services/repe_context.py' (line 9 in summary.md). The IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md mentions 'backend/app/services/repe_context.py' multiple times, showing correct identification of the backend service location.

expectations.text: Skill locates backend/app/routes/repe.py and the relevant service file
expectations.evidence: Both files are correctly identified: summary.md line 231 references 'backend/app/routes/repe.py', and the service file 'backend/app/services/repe_context.py' is identified throughout the documentation. README.md line 225 lists 'Problem: backend/app/services/repe_context.py lines 116-213' and 'Endpoint: backend/app/routes/repe.py lines 310-360'.

expectations.text: Skill adds a pytest test using FakeCursor that covers the null-binding case
expectations.evidence: proposed_test.py contains 4 pytest tests using FakeCursor fixture. The test 'test_context_resolver_auto_creates_and_sets_binding_found_true' (line 25-85) specifically tests the null-binding case where: environment exists, no explicit binding, heuristic fails, and auto-create is enabled. The test explicitly mocks FakeCursor with push_result calls (lines 41-53) to simulate the scenario.

expectations.text: Skill runs make test-backend and reports the result
expectations.evidence: No evidence of actually running tests. The IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md (lines 88-102) DOCUMENTS what test commands should be run (e.g., 'pytest tests/test_repe_context.py::test_context_resolver_auto_creates_and_sets_binding_found_true -v'), but there is no actual test execution output, no pytest results, and no 'make test-backend' output in the outputs directory.

expectations.text: Skill deploys to Railway using railway up --service authentic-sparkle
expectations.evidence: No evidence of deployment. The IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md (lines 122-138) describes deployment steps as documentation (git add, git commit, git push, monitor Railway logs), but there are no actual Railway deployment logs, no deployment confirmation, and no output from 'railway up' command in the outputs directory.

expectations.text: Skill polls Railway deployment status before running smoke tests
expectations.evidence: No evidence of polling deployment status. The smoke_test.sh script (lines 230-236) includes a connectivity check to verify the backend is reachable, but there is no output showing this was actually executed or that deployment status was monitored. The script is provided as a template, not as executed output.

expectations.text: Skill curl-smokes /api/repe/context with the production seed env_id and business_id
expectations.evidence: No evidence of actual curl executions against production. The smoke_test.sh script (lines 56-115) provides curl commands and expected behavior, but there is no output showing actual curl requests being made or their responses. Lines 29-35 show seed IDs are defined in the script, but no execution output is present. The QUICKSTART.md (lines 69-78) shows example curl commands but these are not executed outputs.
